howItWorks.intro: This credit card payoff calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Interest = Total Paid - Original Balance
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How is payoff time calculated?
faq.answer: Using the formula: months = -ln(1 - r×B/P) / ln(1+r), where r is monthly rate, B is balance, P is payment.

faq.question: What happens if I only pay the minimum?
faq.answer: Minimum payments (usually 1-2% of balance) can take 15-30 years to pay off and cost 2-3x the original balance in interest.

faq.question: Should I pay more than the minimum?
faq.answer: Yes. Even $50 extra per month can save years and thousands in interest. Always pay as much as you can above the minimum.

faq.question: What is a good credit card APR?
faq.answer: Average credit card APR in 2026 is about 21-23%. Excellent credit may qualify for 15-18%. Balance transfer cards offer 0% intro APR for 12-21 months.
